# Burn-scar inference vs. ICNF Áreas Ardidas — overlap crosscheck

<!-- generated by: scripts/09_burn_scar_audit.py at b38c6dd0fbaf18e0a6f478c3cedb6b7a0a2746b5 -->

COG: `outputs/cogs/burn_scar_20260610T072820Z.tif` (run `20260610T072820Z`, model `ibm-nasa-geospatial/Prithvi-EO-2.0-300M-BurnScars` @ `a3f2c410`).
Overlap window: **2025-06-09 .. 2025-12-31** (COG trailing window ∩ ICNF vintage, MapServer layer 20).

| metric | value |
|---|---|
| valid pixels | 8713084 |
| ICNF polygons in overlap window | 16 |
| burned pixels (rasterized) | 4341 |
| burned base rate | 0.00050 |
| Spearman ρ (probability vs. burned mask) | 0.0164 |
| top-decile lift (burned rate in top 10% ÷ base rate) | 3.25× |

Both rasters describe burn scars of fires that already happened; the
correlation measures how well the model's *relative* scores rank known
burned area within this AOI and window. It is not a calibration claim
and not a forecast skill claim.

## Domain-shift sanity check (close-out caveat #2)

Inference agreement against the three largest ICNF-2025 perimeters in the
wider region, truth = union of all in-window mapped burns per inference box:

<!-- generated by: scripts/09_burn_scar_sanity.py at 619807b5ed474b32474e8e041e19cd0bf403cca2 (outputs/logs/burn_scar_sanity_20260610_v2.log) -->

| OBJECTID | concelho | area (ha) | fire | S2 window | truth polys | scenes | IoU@0.5 | precision | recall | mean prob in/out |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 112769 | Arganil | 65853 | 2025-08-13..2025-08-25 | 2025-06-29..2025-10-09 | 13 | 51 | 0.746 | 0.747 | 0.999 | 0.993 / 0.299 |
| 111188 | Arouca | 4519 | 2025-07-28..2025-07-28 | 2025-06-13..2025-09-11 | 6 | 38 | 0.721 | 0.726 | 0.992 | 0.969 / 0.287 |
| 111504 | Castro Daire | 2074 | 2025-09-19..2025-09-20 | 2025-08-05..2025-11-04 | 3 | 31 | 0.671 | 0.682 | 0.977 | 0.958 / 0.205 |

## Interpretation (read before using `recent_burn_share_12mo`)

1. **The model transfers.** On real fires with substantial burned area the
   US-HLS-trained checkpoint recovers 98–100 % of ICNF-mapped burn at
   IoU 0.67–0.75, with a wide score separation inside vs. outside
   perimeters (≈0.96–0.99 vs. ≈0.21–0.30). This is the load-bearing
   evidence for the feature.
2. **The pilot-AOI Spearman is uninformative, not alarming.** Only ~43 ha
   of ICNF-mapped burn fell inside the pilot AOI during the overlap window
   (burned base rate 0.0005). At that prevalence a pixel-level rank
   correlation is dominated by ties and noise; the top-decile lift (3.25×)
   is the meaningful readout and shows positive rank association. Re-run
   after the 2026 vintage publishes, or on an AOI with a major in-window
   fire, before quoting a headline correlation.
3. **Max-composite inflation caveat.** The pilot raster's median score is
   ≈0.47 because a 179-scene max-composite keeps each pixel's highest
   single-scene score: any one-scene false positive sticks. Stage 2 must
   not read the composite as areal burn share at face value with the 0.5
   threshold; treat thresholded shares as *relative* rank inputs (which is
   all `config/exposure_score.yaml` requires) and revisit the threshold in
   WU-6 if ranked shares saturate.

All values above are relative model scores for *burn scars* — post-event
spectral signatures of fires that already happened. Nothing here is a
calibrated probability, an ignition prediction, or a forecast.

## WU-10 validation — remediation results

<!-- generated by: scripts/16_burn_scar_validate.py at 229b0238bd45295d6d3fb199ac250397927f560f-dirty -->

COG: `/home/nls/Documents/dev/wildfire-exposure-eo/outputs/cogs/burn_scar_wu10multi_p85_20260615T173557Z.tif` (run `20260615T173557Z_p85`, reducer `p85`, model `ibm-nasa-geospatial/Prithvi-EO-2.0-300M-BurnScars` @ `a3f2c410`).
ICNF perimeters in matched window: 16. True-negative sample: 10000 pixels, fuel-class-stratified (seed 42).

Burn SCARS detected — post-event spectral signatures of fires that already happened. The values below describe how many pixels score *above threshold* and how that set overlaps ICNF-mapped *matched area*. Nothing here is a calibrated probability, a risk, or a forecast.

| metric | baseline (WU-1 COG) | remediated COG |
|---|---|---|
| frac_above_05 | 0.473 | 0.113 |
| median score | 0.470 | 0.109 |
| FPR@0.5 | n/a (not measured in WU-1) | 0.1114 |
| precision@0.5 | n/a | 0.1256 |
| recall@0.5 | n/a | 0.0369 |
| F1@0.5 | n/a | 0.0570 |
| IoU@0.5 | n/a | 0.0293 |

Predicted-positive rate by fuel class (drivers of false positives):

| fuel class | valid pixels | predicted positive | rate |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | 3270040 | 327180 | 0.1001 |
| 1 | 180204 | 20273 | 0.1125 |
| 3 | 1544 | 0 | 0.0000 |
| 4 | 24649 | 96 | 0.0039 |
| 5 | 708334 | 106402 | 0.1502 |
| 9 | 1306517 | 245751 | 0.1881 |
| 10 | 2583671 | 212710 | 0.0823 |

### Reducer choice (data-driven, WU-10)

All four candidate composites were emitted from a single inference pass over
the same 109-scene in-season stack (`scripts/16_burn_scar_multireducer_run.py`,
run `20260615T173557Z`); each was scored by `scripts/16_burn_scar_validate.py`
(seed 42). Summary at the feature's 0.5 threshold, plus the PR-curve readout:

| reducer | frac_above_05 | best-F1 (thr) | recall @best-F1 | recall@0.3 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| max (baseline reducer) | 0.372 | 0.471 (0.10) | 0.901 | 0.421 |
| **p85 (chosen)** | **0.113** | **0.365 (0.10)** | **0.492** | **0.161** |
| median | 0.057 | 0.276 (0.10) | 0.282 | 0.056 |
| consensus_3 | 0.083 | 0.029 (0.10) | 0.018 | 0.018 |

`p85` is the recommended reducer. It cuts the over-prediction wash 3.3× versus
`max` (frac_above_05 0.372 → 0.113; median score 0.353 → 0.109, toward the
~0.04 single-scene baseline) while Pareto-dominating `max` at matched recall:
`p85`@0.10 (recall 0.492, precision 0.290, FPR 0.523) beats `max`@0.30
(recall 0.421, precision 0.251, FPR 0.546) on all three. `median` and
`consensus_3` over-correct — they suppress the wash further but collapse recall
on the genuine ICNF-2025 scars, the trade WU-10 explicitly avoids. Absolute
recall at 0.5 is low for every reducer because only ~43 ha of ICNF-mapped burn
fell inside the pilot AOI in the matched window (base rate 0.0005); the PR
curve, not the single 0.5 point, is the readout. Fuel classes 9 and 5 drive
most of the residual predicted-positive area (cross-tab above) — the basis for
the FLAG B land-cover-guard question in `prompts/_HIL.md`.

The remediated COG remains a CANDIDATE under `outputs/` only: no live artefact
(R2 display COG, `stac/burn-scar-recent/` item, exposure parquet,
`docs/validation_report.md`) has been changed. Adopting it requires human
sign-off on FLAG A — see `prompts/_HIL.md`.

Burn SCARS detected — post-event spectral signatures of fires that already
happened. Not ignition prediction. All values are relative model scores, never
calibrated probabilities or forecasts.

## WU-10 tiling artifact — diagnosis and de-grid

<!-- generated by: scripts/16_burn_scar_gridmetric.py + scripts/16_burn_scar_multireducer_run.py at 508f835e44d94d1faf4f48b5dd82061d7167e9b7 -->

The p85 reducer cut the over-prediction wash ~3.3× but left a faint *grid* in the
composite: a regular lattice of square cells. This section records the confirmed
root cause and the de-grid fix, both of which are script-reproducible.

### Root cause — ViT per-crop centre bias × MAX, phase-locked across scenes

The grid is the model's per-crop **positional bias**, not a display or
COG-blocking artifact. terratorch `tiled_inference` slides a fixed axis-aligned
crop lattice (crop 512 px, stride 448 px, origin 0,0) over every scene. Each
Prithvi/ViT 512 px crop carries a tent-shaped class-1 response — high at the crop
centre (~0.55), low at the crop border (~0.06), roughly 5× core/border. Because
the lattice is **phase-locked** to the same UTM grid across all ~179 scenes, the
per-pixel composite stacks every tent at the same pixels: under `max` they
saturate into squares; under `p85` a fainter but still periodic grid remains.

The evidence is the composite's autocorrelation **period**. Measured by
`scripts/16_burn_scar_gridmetric.py` on the pre-de-grid pilot composites
(EPSG:4326), the dominant off-origin autocorrelation peak sits at an
**anisotropic** lag — row ≈ 504 px, col ≈ 384 px on the p85 composite (row ≈ 487,
col ≈ 384 on `max`). That anisotropic period is the **reprojected inference
stride** (448 px in UTM 10 m, stretched anisotropically by the warp to 4326), not
the isotropic 512-px COG block — i.e. the artifact is created during inference,
not during display.

Three competing hypotheses were **refuted by measurement**, leaving the
positional-bias explanation:

- **H1 — nodata/zero-fill out-of-distribution.** Refuted: the correlation between
  a crop's masked-fraction and its mean class-1 score is ~0; masked crops do not
  saturate. (The zero-fill was nonetheless tightened to a per-band-mean fill as
  hygiene; see below.)
- **H2 — blend seams between overlapping crops.** Refuted: the cosine feather
  terratorch applies in the overlap works; the grid period matches the *stride*,
  not the seam spacing.
- **H3 — per-tile normalisation.** Refuted: the normalisation means/stds are
  global constants read once from the model config, applied before tiling.

### De-grid fix — deterministic per-scene crop-origin jitter

The phase-lock — not the tent itself — is what a percentile composite cannot
average out. The fix breaks it by jittering the crop-grid **origin** per scene:
before `tiled_inference`, the normalised scene array is reflect-padded on the
top/left by a deterministic per-scene `(dy, dx)` in `[0, stride)`, derived from
seed 42 mixed with the STAC item id via blake2b (reproducible, order-independent
— `burn_scar.scene_origin_offset`). The offset is inverted by cropping the
returned probability back to the scene extent, so every scene stays on the common
grid. The tent now lands at different pixels each scene, so the p85 composite
averages it into the background instead of reinforcing it. The choice is exposed
as `config/burn_scar.yaml: inference.tile_origin_jitter` (default `false` for
backward compatibility; the shipped config sets `true`) and recorded in
`BurnScarRun` provenance (`tile_origin_jitter`, `tile_size`, `tile_stride`).

This adds **no extra forward passes** (same crops per scene, only the origin
moves) so GPU cost is unchanged; the stride is left at 448. A hygiene change rode
along: masked pixels are now filled with the per-band reflectance mean (→ ~0.0
after normalisation, neutral) instead of `0.0` reflectance (→ −mean/std, a
strongly OOD value). Not the grid's cause, but a cleaner no-data treatment.

### De-grid result (grid metric A/B + recall preservation)

Both composites come from one inference pass over the same 109-scene in-season
stack; the grid metric is the high-passed 2D-FFT autocorrelation peak and the
`grid_power_ratio` (share of high-passed power at the stride frequency), plus the
saturated-square fraction `frac_ge_095`. BEFORE = the p85 pilot without jitter
(`burn_scar_wu10multi_p85_20260615T173557Z`); AFTER = p85 with the de-grid
(`burn_scar_wu10degrid_p85_*`).

| metric | BEFORE p85 (no jitter) | AFTER p85 (de-grid) |
|---|---|---|
| autocorr peak (2D) | 0.467 @ lag (2, 505) px | 0.204 @ lag (0, 269) px |
| autocorr row-lag / col-lag | 504 px / 384 px | 269 px / 357 px |
| grid_power_ratio | 0.069 | 0.015 |
| frac_ge_095 (saturation) | 0.0054 | 0.0012 |
| frac_above_05 | 0.113 | 0.114 |
| recall@0.10 (ICNF-2025) | 0.492 | 0.855 |
| recall@0.15 (ICNF-2025) | 0.388 | 0.638 |
| recall@0.20 (ICNF-2025) | 0.312 | 0.433 |
| recall@0.30 (ICNF-2025) | 0.161 | 0.165 |

The de-grid strongly attenuates the periodic grid while *preserving* — in fact
improving — recall on the genuine ICNF-2025 scars across the operating range:

- The stride-locked periodicity collapses. The autocorrelation peak drops from
  0.467 to 0.204 and its lag is no longer pinned to the 504/384-px crop-stride
  lattice (it scatters to 269/357 px — the residual is the scene's genuine
  spatial structure, not the phase-locked tent). The spectral `grid_power_ratio`
  falls 78 % (0.069 → 0.015) and the saturated-square fraction falls 78 %
  (0.0054 → 0.0012).
- The overall score distribution is essentially unchanged (`frac_above_05`
  0.113 → 0.114): the jitter *redistributes* the tent across scenes rather than
  removing signal, exactly as a percentile composite should consume it.
- Recall on the real ICNF-2025 perimeters is preserved and improves at the
  meaningful low thresholds (recall@0.10 0.492 → 0.855; @0.15 0.388 → 0.638;
  @0.20 0.312 → 0.433; @0.30 0.161 → 0.165). Removing the phase-locked
  false-positive grid — together with the per-band-mean no-data fill — lets the
  genuine scar signal stand out instead of competing with the artifact. FPR@0.5
  is unchanged (0.111 → 0.115). Absolute recall at 0.5 is negligible for both
  (0.037 → 0.020) because the ICNF base rate inside the pilot AOI is ~0.0005; the
  PR curve, not the single 0.5 point, is the readout (see the reducer-choice note
  above). The de-grid does NOT trade the grid for missing genuine scars.

The de-gridded composite came from one inference pass over the same 109-scene
in-season stack (`scripts/16_burn_scar_multireducer_run.py`, run
`20260615T192025Z`, `tile_origin_jitter=true`, stride 448, seed 42); the grid
metric is `scripts/16_burn_scar_gridmetric.py` and the recall sweep is
`scripts/16_burn_scar_validate.py` (PR-curve JSON
`outputs/diagnostics/16_pr_curve_degrid_p85.json`).

The de-gridded COG remains a CANDIDATE under `outputs/` only — no live artefact
(R2 display COG, `stac/burn-scar-recent/` item, exposure parquet,
`docs/validation_report.md`) has been changed. Adopting it requires human sign-off
on FLAG A — see `prompts/_HIL.md`.

Burn SCARS detected — post-event spectral signatures of fires that already
happened. Not ignition prediction. All values are relative model scores, never
calibrated probabilities or forecasts.

## WU-10 detection validation — multi-year persistent-scar truth

<!-- generated by: scripts/16_burn_scar_multiyear_validate.py at eccf1a03fea4734ac621df975671ed581bb0544e (outputs/diagnostics/16_multiyear_detection_20260615T192025Z.json) -->

COG: `outputs/cogs/burn_scar_20260615T192025Z.tif` (de-grid p85, run
`20260615T192025Z`). Truth = ICNF Áreas Ardidas perimeters by vintage layer
(2021→15, 2022→17, 2023→18, 2024→19, 2025→20), rasterised on the COG grid
(both sides explicitly EPSG:4326). Full-grid evaluation over all valid AOI
pixels (seed 42).

| truth window | AOI coverage | best-F1 (thr) | precision @0.5 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2025 only | 0.1 % | 0.030 (thr 0.90) | 0.003 |
| **2023–2025** | **24.8 %** | **0.644 (thr 0.30)** | **0.806** |
| 2021–2025 | 28.4 % | 0.619 (thr 0.30) | 0.813 |

The 2025-only window covers ≈ 95 ha (0.1 % of the AOI) — far too sparse to grade
a detector; at that prevalence even a good detector scores best-F1 0.03. The
**2023–2025** window (dominated by the major September-2024 Aveiro-region fires,
≈ 22 900 ha inside the AOI) is the correct detection truth: against it the
de-grid p85 layer reaches best-F1 **0.644** at threshold 0.30 (precision 0.60,
recall 0.70), precision **0.81** at 0.5 and 0.90 at 0.7. The over-prediction
ratio at thr 0.30 is ≈ 1.2× (recall/precision), i.e. approximately calibrated in
areal terms.

**Why multi-year truth is the correct evaluation for a *detection* layer.** Burn
scars are post-event spectral signatures that persist for **years**, so the
trailing-window imagery legitimately shows scars from earlier-vintage fires.
Grading the detector only against the single latest ICNF vintage understates it.
This is distinct from the **forecasting** exposure score (WU-7), where a hard
**temporal-leakage gate** binds: that score must not see any imagery acquired
after the perimeter date, because predicting a known-burned pixel from its own
fire's smoke is leakage. **Leakage-safety binds the forecasting score, not
detection** — so the multi-year detection table above deliberately applies no
leakage gate, while the WU-7 validation report keeps it.

Burn SCARS detected — post-event spectral signatures of fires that already
happened. Not ignition prediction. All values are relative model scores, never
calibrated probabilities or forecasts.
